title: bee worktree merge --id <id>: merge-back from main + configured verify as the semantic-conflict alarm (D8)
action: Add mergeFeatureWorktree(mainRoot, {id, cleanup}) to skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/worktree-store.mjs: typed zero-mutation refusals for unknown/ungranted id, merge invoked from a non-ordinary checkout, id resolving to the caller's own worktree (advisor R5), dirty MAIN tree, dirty WORKTREE tree — dirty = git status --porcelain WITHOUT --ignored, gitignored .bee runtime files never count (decision D8a/advisor R1; test merge succeeds against a worktree with fully-bootstrapped store + committed work), detached HEAD or wrong branch in the worktree; git calls via argv arrays only (R2). Then git merge --no-ff wt/<slug> in MAIN; textual conflict -> typed MERGE_CONFLICT (git state left for the human); clean merge -> run readConfig(mainRoot).commands.verify (state.mjs readConfig; falsy -> verify:'skipped'); green -> {ok, merged, verify:'green'}; --cleanup (D8b: unconditional on green, without flag only a reported suggestion, never a prompt) orders: git worktree remove FIRST then git branch -d wt/<slug> (-d never -D; branch still checked out otherwise — advisor R4); worktree remove policy: --force allowed for the disposable gitignored store, but refuse cleanup when tracked-modified or untracked tracked-path files remain; test cleanup against a USED worktree. Red verify -> typed MERGE_VERIFY_RED with output tail + the sentence naming it the semantic-conflict alarm; merge commit never rolled back. CLI verb worktree.merge: registry entry (valid schema + deprecated key) + handler + UPDATE worktreeUsageFallback to 'Use: register, list, unregister, new, merge.' (bijection coupling — anchors report #1). Tests in scripts/test_worktree_cli.mjs: green path (fixture verify in config.json commands), MERGE_VERIFY_RED via textual-clean merge flipping a fixture test red (merge commit intact), MERGE_CONFLICT path, dirty-main + dirty-worktree refusals, bootstrapped-store-not-dirty case, cleanup on a used worktree incl. refusal path, own-worktree refusal; bijection row in test_bee_cli.mjs. Same 4-way mirrors as wsr-1.

truths: merge refuses typed and zero-mutation on unknown id, dirty main, dirty worktree, detached/wrong-branch worktree
a textual-clean merge that breaks a fixture test yields MERGE_VERIFY_RED with output tail while the merge commit stays intact (the semantic-conflict alarm)
a textual conflict yields typed MERGE_CONFLICT with git conflict state left for the human
cleanup runs only after green verify, uses branch -d (never -D), and refuses when the worktree still has untracked/modified files
registry+example bijection row exists and all four byte-mirror trees are identical

verify: node scripts/test_worktree_cli.mjs && node scripts/test_worktree_store.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs

trace.outcome: bee worktree merge shipped: typed zero-mutation refusals, D8a dirty definition, MERGE_CONFLICT / MERGE_VERIFY_RED semantic alarm (merge never rolled back), D8b/D8c cleanup ordering with -d only; independent verify green on integration branch e0759a6
trace.verification_evidence:
# wsr-2 — done report (bee worktree merge)

Worker: wsr2-worker (sonnet, isolated harness worktree, base 3c56b7a). Worker
commit `d8957fd`, merged into integration branch `wt/worktree-session-routing`
as `e0759a6`. 2026-07-18.

## What shipped
- `mergeFeatureWorktree(mainRoot, {id, cleanup, verifyCommand})` in
  `skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/worktree-store.mjs`: typed zero-mutation
  refusals (`WorktreeMergeError`): `WORKTREE_MERGE_INVALID_ID`,
  `WORKTREE_MERGE_CALLER_NOT_ORDINARY` (covers own-worktree self-merge, advisor
  R5), `WORKTREE_MERGE_UNKNOWN_ID`, `WORKTREE_MERGE_MAIN_DIRTY`,
  `WORKTREE_MERGE_WORKTREE_DIRTY` (D8a: porcelain without --ignored — a
  bootstrapped gitignored .bee store is never dirty, proven by test),
  `WORKTREE_MERGE_DETACHED_HEAD`, `WORKTREE_MERGE_BRANCH_MISMATCH`.
- `git merge --no-ff` in MAIN; textual conflict → `MERGE_CONFLICT` (git state
  left, no rollback); clean merge → caller-passed verifyCommand (CLI resolves
  `readConfig(mainRoot).commands.verify`; lib stays dependency-free, matching
  wsr-1's structure) → red = `MERGE_VERIFY_RED` with output tail named as the
  semantic-conflict alarm, merge commit never rolled back.
- `--cleanup` (D8b/D8c): unconditional on green/skipped verify; order worktree
  remove → branch -d (never -D) → removeGrant; typed cleanup refusal when
  tracked-modified/untracked files remain (merge stays ok); never after
  conflict/red; skipped-verify cleanup carries the D8c warning (decision
  d5a00839).
- CLI verb `worktree merge` + registry entry + usage fallback "Use: register,
  list, unregister, new, merge." (bijection coupling); `cleanup` in
  FLAG_ALONE_BOOLEANS; 4-way byte mirrors synced (md5-verified by worker,
  test-verified below).

## Verification (fresh command output)
Independent orchestrator re-run on the integration branch after merge e0759a6:

```
node scripts/test_worktree_cli.mjs   -> SUMMARY: 90/90 passed
node scripts/test_worktree_store.mjs -> SUMMARY: 22/22 passed
node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs -> 170 passed, 0 failed
node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs     ->
PASS test_lib_mirror: templates/lib and .bee/bin/lib are byte-identical (19 files)
PASS test_lib_mirror: runtime-derived hook inventory is byte-identical (10 files)
```

## Diff (merge e0759a6)
16 files changed, 2248 insertions(+), 13 deletions(-).

## Deviations (accepted)
- Own-worktree self-merge collapses into `WORKTREE_MERGE_CALLER_NOT_ORDINARY`
  (no separate code) — advisor-confirmed single-code interpretation.
- Cleanup-on-skipped-verify resolved as D8c with mandatory warning (decision
  d5a00839) — advisor-confirmed gap resolution, not literal D8 text.
- Known cosmetic: one unused variable (`grantsFileA`, test_worktree_cli.mjs
  ~line 476) flagged by the linter — queued for wsr-3 to drop.
